seed_instruction: Write an article titled "How to overcome public speaking anxiety."
org_constraint: Structure the article in JSON format with the following keys: {"title": String, "introduction": String, "body": Array of Strings, "conclusion": String}.
new_constraint: Structure the article in Markdown format, including at least three level-2 headers.
instruction: Write an article titled "How to overcome public speaking anxiety." Structure the article in JSON format with the following keys: {"title": String, "introduction": String, "body": Array of Strings, "conclusion": String}. Structure the article in Markdown format, including at least three level-2 headers.
response:
```json
{
  "title": "How to Overcome Public Speaking Anxiety",
  "introduction": "Public speaking is one of the most common fears worldwide, often ranking higher than the fear of heights or even spiders. This phenomenon, known as glossophobia, can cause physical symptoms like trembling, sweating, and a racing heart. However, public speaking is a skill that can be mastered with the right techniques. By shifting your mindset and practicing specific physical and mental strategies, you can transform your anxiety into channeled energy and deliver a confident, impactful presentation.",
  "body": [
    "## Master Your Physiology\n\nBefore you even step onto the stage, your body's 'fight or flight' response is likely kicking in. To counteract this, focus on controlled breathing. Practice 'box breathing'—inhaling for four seconds, holding for four, exhaling for four, and holding for four. This signals to your nervous system that you are safe. Additionally, use power posing; standing in a confident stance for two minutes before your speech can lower cortisol levels and increase feelings of dominance and confidence.",
    "## Preparation is the Antidote to Fear\n\nMuch of public speaking anxiety stems from a fear of the unknown or the fear of forgetting your lines. The best way to mitigate this is through rigorous preparation. Instead of memorizing a script word-for-word—which can lead to panic if you lose your place—focus on mastering the key concepts and transitions. Create a structured outline and practice your delivery multiple times. Familiarity with your material builds 'muscle memory,' allowing you to speak more naturally and reducing the cognitive load during the actual performance.",
    "## Reframe Your Mindset\n\nAnxiety and excitement are physiologically very similar; both involve a racing heart and heightened senses. Instead of telling yourself to 'calm down,' try telling yourself that you are 'excited.' This cognitive reframing shifts your perspective from a threat to an opportunity. Furthermore, remember that the audience is generally on your side; they want you to succeed because they are there to learn from you. Shift your focus away from your own performance and toward the value you are providing to the listeners."
  ],
  "conclusion": "Overcoming public speaking anxiety is not about eliminating nerves entirely, but about learning to manage them. By mastering your physical responses, preparing thoroughly, and reframing your internal narrative, you can turn a daunting task into a rewarding experience. With consistent practice and patience, you will find that your voice has the power to inspire, inform, and lead."
}
```<turn|>
